name: Black Brugo
bio:
Physical Appearance: Orsimer (Orc) male. Wears heavy armor - boots, cuirass, and helmet - with lower class pants underneath. Sometimes carries a shield. His equipment is battle-worn from his life as a bandit chieftain.
Race: Orsimer
Personality Keywords: menace, outlaw, notorious, ruthless, criminal
Role: Black Brugo is the Chieftain of the Black Bow Bandits, a marauder gang that has been terrorizing County Leyawiin. His gang is known for their signature weapon - black bows - which gives them their name. He leads his bandits in attacking innocent travelers throughout the county. Count Marius Caro considers him enough of a menace that he has ordered Brugo's execution for his crimes.
Base Location Information: Black Brugo operates in the wilderness between Border Watch and the Ayleid ruin of Telepe, where he hides out with his companions Alonzo and Roxy Aric. The gang uses Telepe as a regular stopover point.
Daily Routine: After spending the day in the wilderness ambushing travelers, Brugo and his gang make their way to Telepe around 8pm, arriving after about an hour's travel. They spend the entire night at the ruin - likely collecting their stolen goods and dividing their take - before heading back outside at 6am to return to their base in the wilderness.
The Black Bow: Brugo carries one of the black bows that give his gang their notorious reputation, though he also wields a longsword or claymore in combat. He keeps the key to an Ayleid coffer in Telepe on his person, along with healing potions - necessities for his dangerous lifestyle.
Past Connections: Mazoga the Orc knew Brugo from her past before she became "Sir Mazoga," when she was involved with outlaws herself. She once split shares with him, though she now calls him "a jerk" and seems to have no loyalty remaining toward her former associate.
Personality - The Bandit Chief: Black Brugo has built a reputation as a ruthless outlaw leader whose gang has plagued County Leyawiin long enough that the Count has placed a bounty on him and his followers. His nightly routine of returning to Telepe suggests an organized criminal operation rather than random violence. The fact that even a former criminal associate like Mazoga readily turns on him suggests he may have earned few friends even among fellow outlaws.
author: Bethesda
